_comment:
Per-integration CEL rules for process auto-discovery, evaluated by analysis/scripts/process_cel_eval.py. Fields available in CEL: process.name (from /proc/<pid>/status Name, 15-character kernel limit — note this matches workloadmeta.Process.Name which the agent populates the same way), process.cmdline (joined cmdline), process.args (cmdline split — see caveat below). Style: prefer `process.name == 'X'` when `comm` uniquely identifies the binary; otherwise combine name with `args.exists(a, ...)` or `X in process.args` to match individual argv elements. Avoid broad cmdline substring matches: 'raylet' as a cmdline substring matches any process whose cmdline references the raylet socket path, not just the raylet binary itself. Each rule below is overridable; when an integration is omitted here the script falls back to manifest.json process_signatures.

Caveat: the collected data stores cmdline as a single space-joined string (null bytes replaced with spaces). For most processes this is fine because argv elements don't contain internal spaces. For processes that rewrite argv[0] to include spaces (e.g. nginx's 'nginx: master process nginx'), the args list this script derives by splitting on whitespace will differ from the production argv. For those cases rules use process.cmdline.contains(...) rather than args.

airflow.notes: Airflow scheduler renames argv[0] to 'airflow scheduler ...'; gunicorn does similar. Both target the same airflow check.

celery.notes: Celery workers either rename argv[0] to 'celery' or launch as python /path/to/celery.

ceph.notes: Ceph daemons are distinct binaries — match by name.

citrix_hypervisor.notes: Test fixture is a python mock (`python server.py run -h 0.0.0.0`). Real Citrix Hypervisor would need a different rule against the actual XenServer toolstack.

clickhouse.notes: comm is 15-char-truncated from 'clickhouse-server', which is unique enough on its own.

confluent_platform.notes: Confluent Platform: brokers (kafka.Kafka), zookeeper, schema registry, ksql, control center, kafka-rest.

consul.notes: Consul agent — name + explicit subcommand to avoid matching other consul invocations.

couchbase.notes: Couchbase erlang VM. Targets all 3 beam.smp roles (babysitter, ns_server, ns_couchdb) that share the /opt/couchbase prefix in argv[0]; a tighter rule could pick a single role.

elastic.notes: Test fixture runs OpenSearch; production may run Elasticsearch — accept either main class.

hive.notes: Apache Hive runs HiveServer2 (SQL query engine) and the Metastore (catalog) as separate JVMs. Disco labels both as the generic 'hadoop' generated_name; the integration's conf.yaml.example documents 'HiveServer2 or Hive Metastore JMX host' as the target, so users configure one instance per role. Disambiguate via the -Dproc_<role> JVM arg. Excludes HDFS NameNode/DataNode in the same env — those belong to hdfs_namenode/hdfs_datanode integrations.

mapreduce.notes: Matches NameNode, DataNode, ResourceManager, NodeManager, JobHistoryServer, etc.

marathon.notes: Test fixture launches marathon as 'java -jar marathon-assembly-1.3.0.jar' rather than via the documented mesosphere.marathon.Main class.

n8n.notes: node /usr/local/bin/n8n — comm is unreliable (shows up as 'MainThread' here).

nginx.notes: nginx rewrites argv[0] to include spaces ('nginx: master process nginx -g daemon off;'). The space-split args this script derives doesn't preserve that single argv element, so use cmdline.contains for the role marker.

php_fpm.notes: php-fpm rewrites argv[0] to 'php-fpm: master process ...' (same pattern as nginx).

postgres.notes: PostgreSQL master is launched with -D <datadir> or -c config_file=. Background workers (checkpointer, walwriter, etc.) rewrite argv[0] to 'postgres: <role>' and don't carry these flags.

prefect.notes: 'prefect server start' and 'prefect worker start' — target both.

rabbitmq.notes: rabbitmq runs as Erlang beam.smp. Distinguish from couchbase/riak beam.smp via the rabbit-specific path/cookie in args.

ray.notes: Match the native raylet and gcs_server binaries explicitly. The python client.server is launched as 'python -m ray.util.client.server ...', so check args without falling back to a 'raylet' substring (which would also match worker processes that reference the raylet socket path).

riak.notes: Distinguish riak's beam.smp from couchbase/rabbitmq via the riak install path.

sonarqube.notes: SonarQube's launcher (sonar-application) forks three child JVMs with distinct generated_names: WebServer, CeServer, and embedded Elasticsearch. The manifest's process_signatures target WebServer + CeServer specifically — those are the JMX-exposing components the integration monitors. The launcher and the embedded ES are not targets of this integration. Expected post-CEL: 2 instances per SonarQube deployment, matching the manifest signature.

sonatype_nexus.notes: Sonatype Nexus runs via a Spring Boot launcher; identify by the install prefix in JVM args (LogFile, classpath) since the Nexus main class isn't directly in argv.

esxi.notes: Test fixture uses VMware's vcsim simulator. Real ESXi would be queried over the API, not by host process.

go_expvar.notes: go_expvar targets any Go binary publishing expvars; test fixture binary is named test_expvar.

hudi.notes: Hudi test fixture runs as Spark — same predicate as the spark integration.

nfsstat.notes: nfsstat is a check against /proc/net/rpc, not a daemon. The test fixture provides rpcbind; this rule matches what's present, though the production integration probably doesn't rely on process discovery.

teamcity.notes: Test fixture runs a mock server, not real TeamCity — the actual cmdline is org.mockserver.cli.Main. This rule won't match in the fixture but represents what the real check would target.

temporal.notes: comm is exactly 'temporal-server' (15 chars, at the limit).

tls.notes: tls test fixture serves TLS via nginx — same rule as nginx.

tomcat.notes: Embedded tomcat in the test fixture (classpath contains tomcat-embed-core-*.jar); standard tomcat uses the org.apache.catalina.startup.Bootstrap main class.

traffic_server.notes: Apache Traffic Server rewrites argv[0] to [TS_MAIN] / [TS_MANAGER]; comm doesn't match. The real binary appears as the second argv element after argv[0]. Use cmdline.contains on the binary path.

vault.notes: Vault server (distinguishes from `vault status`, `vault read`, etc. invocations).

activemq_xml.notes: Same target as activemq — the _xml variant scrapes a different endpoint format.

apache.notes: Canonical pre-fork master/worker — algorithm correctly dedupes workers to the master.

gunicorn.notes: gunicorn rewrites argv[0] to 'gunicorn: master [app-name]'; comm is 15-char-truncated to 'gunicorn: maste'. Algorithm correctly dedupes worker children.

http_check.notes: http_check is an active HTTP probe; it does not target a specific host process. No CEL match is appropriate — the integration is configured via remote URLs.

impala.notes: Impala runs three components — impalad (comm='impalad'), statestored (comm='statestored'), and catalogd (comm='main', cmdline starts with /opt/impala/bin/catalogd). Manifest signatures list all three.

kong.notes: Kong is OpenResty/nginx with the kong prefix in its cmdline. Use cmdline.contains because nginx rewrites argv[0].

mesos_slave.notes: Test fixture only captured zookeeper; mesos-slave itself didn't show up. The rule reflects what the integration would target in production.

openmetrics.notes: openmetrics is a generic Prometheus scraper; in this test env it points at a real prometheus binary.
